mission.title: fluent-bit: Loki Output - remove_keys skipped if used in labels
mission.description: Loki Output - remove_keys skipped if used in labels. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current fluent-bit deployment
steps.description:
Verify your fluent-bit version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
helm list -n fluent-bit 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working fluent-bit installation.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
Using Loki output, `remove_keys` don't work as expected when values within `remove_keys` are used by `labels`

using `fluent/fluent-bit:1.9.4`

**To Reproduce**

```yaml

steps.title: Apply the fix for Loki Output - remove_keys skipped if used in labels
steps.description:
out_loki tries to remove key/value using flb_mp_accessor_keys_remove.
The API needs a key lists to indicate which key should be removed.

I tested the API and I found that the list should be sorted if it is passed nested key/value.
However the lists is not sorted at current master. It may not work
```yaml
# docker-compose.yaml
version: "3.7"

services:
  fluent-bit:
    image: fluent/fluent-bit
    network_mode: "host"
    volumes:
      - ./fluent-bit.yaml:/fluent-bit/etc/fluent-bit.yaml
    command: "/fluent-bit/bin/fluent-bit -c /fluent-bit/etc/fluent-bit.yaml"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Loki Output - remove_keys skipped if used in labels" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
out_loki tries to remove key/value using flb_mp_accessor_keys_remove.
The API needs a key lists to indicate which key should be removed.

I tested the API and I found that the list should be sorted if it is passed nested key/value.
However the lists is not sorted at current master. It may not work if user passes nested key/value.

This patch is to fix it.
codeSnippets:
# docker-compose.yaml
version: "3.7"

services:
  fluent-bit:
    image: fluent/fluent-bit
    network_mode: "host"
    volumes:
      - ./fluent-bit.yaml:/fluent-bit/etc/fluent-bit.yaml
    command: "/fluent-bit/bin/fluent-bit -c /fluent-bit/etc/fluent-bit.yaml"

# fluent-bit.yaml
env:
  log_level: debug
  flush_interval: 5

pipeline:
  inputs:
  - dummy:
      dummy: '{"log":"blahblah","kubernetes":{"pod_name":"nginx", "namespace_name":"default"}}'

  outputs:
  - loki:
      match: '*'
      auto_kubernetes_labels: off
      labels: pod=$kubernetes['pod_name']
      remove_keys: kubernetes

$ docker-compose up

$ nc -l 3100
POST /loki/api/v1/push HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1:3100
Content-Length: 148
User-Agent: Fluent-Bit
Content-Type: application/json

{"streams":[{"stream":{"pod":"nginx"},"values":[["1654686265055834768","{\"log\":\"blahblah\",\"kubernetes\":{\"namespace_name\":\"default\"}}"]]}]}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
